focus: Slice 7: system-skill-advisor DB and lease layer

notes: Core DDL uses standard CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CHECK constraints, AUTOINCREMENT, FK with ON DELETE CASCADE — all supported by Turso except vec_metadata usage patterns downstream.
better-sqlite3 .pragma() API has no direct Turso equivalent; busy_timeout and foreign_keys must be set via PRAGMA statements or connection options; wal_checkpoint(FULL) may not be available.
sqlite-vec virtual tables (vec0 module) require loadExtension() which Turso does not support; this blocks vector embedding storage entirely.
better-sqlite3 .exec() is synchronous multi-statement; Turso JS driver uses async .execute() or .batch() for multi-statement execution.
better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction API; Turso uses .batch() for transactional multi-statement execution — different API shape.
Read-only handle pattern for shadow scoring; Turso embedded may not support fileMustExist option or separate readonly connections.
better-sqlite3 synchronous Statement objects cached in WeakMap; Turso async driver uses different Statement type — cache pattern must adapt.
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL inside .exec() multi-statement; Turso libSQL may reject PRAGMA in batch/execute or handle differently.
Lease acquisition is a synchronous better-sqlite3 transaction; Turso async .batch() replaces this — critical for single-writer daemon model.
Lease DB has its own busy_timeout via .pragma() API; must migrate to Turso connection-level config.
Opens fresh better-sqlite3 handle per retry; Turso embedded connection pooling may change retry semantics.
PRAGMA quick_check on readonly handle; Turso embedded may not support quick_check or fileMustExist option.
WAL mode pragma with file-system error fallback; Turso has no .pragma() API — this entire pattern must be replaced or removed.
WAL checkpoint is a local-file-only concept; Turso/libSQL uses different replication — this entire function is migration-blocking dead code.
Multi-statement .exec() DDL; Turso supports this but schema migration pattern couples to synchronous better-sqlite3 .exec() semantics.
File-level destructive recovery (rename/rm the .sqlite file); Turso DB is a remote service — file-based corruption recovery is impossible, needs service-level replacement.
Synchronous transaction wrapper over better-sqlite3; Turso/libSQL JS client uses async transactions — this entire block must become async.
Dynamic table name (vec_<dim>) with embedding BLOB upsert; Turso lacks vec0 virtual tables and vector indexes — the entire adapter path is migration-blocking.
Read-only mode via better-sqlite3 constructor flag; Turso connections are inherently read-replicas — the readonly concept maps differently.
WAL fallback error detection pattern; Turso has no WAL mode — this error-handling convention is migration-blocking dead code.
Heartbeat-based lease acquisition in synchronous transaction; the entire single-writer lease model assumes local-file WAL locking — Turso's remote locking model invalidates this design.
Periodic heartbeat UPDATE on lease row; if Turso connection drops, heartbeat fails silently — needs connection-liveness detection absent in current code.
Generic SQLITE_BUSY retry wrapper opening a fresh DB per attempt; Turso has different error codes for contention — the retry predicate must be updated.
Opens daemon state DB with zero pragmas (no WAL, no busy_timeout); relies on caller retry logic — Turso connection pooling semantics would change this.
better-sqlite3 synchronous constructor; libSQL client is async — every call site must become await-aware or use a sync wrapper
WAL mode with isWalFallbackError() fallback logic; Turso handles journaling differently — this branching logic becomes dead code
PRAGMA table_info used for schema introspection in ensureSchemaMigrations; must use libSQL information_schema or equivalent
WAL checkpoint not applicable to Turso's replication model — becomes no-op or error
better-sqlite3 synchronous .transaction() wrapper; Turso/libSQL transaction API is async and structurally different
better-sqlite3 constructor options; libSQL open() takes different option shape — must map readonly/fileMustExist
better-sqlite3 synchronous .prepare()/.get()/.all(); libSQL prepare() is async and returns different statement objects
WeakMap keyed on better-sqlite3 Database instance; libSQL connection type differs, cache strategy needs rework
Separate lease DB with its own Database instances; each open/close cycle must become async under libSQL
Lease DB busy_timeout via .pragma(); critical for single-writer lease protocol — must migrate to PRAGMA SQL statement
Lease DB sets WAL via .exec() raw SQL; WAL semantics differ in Turso embedded replicas — lease contention model may break
Synchronous transaction wrapping lease acquire/reserve logic; must become async — heartbeat loop timing affected
better-sqlite3 RunResult.changes; libSQL execute() returns different result shape — changes field needs mapping
Per-call synchronous Database open/close; runDaemonStateMutation retry loop must become fully async
Read-only probe open; same constructor-options mapping needed as other new Database sites
PRAGMA quick_check for corruption detection; Turso/libSQL embedded mode may not support quick_check — integrity model differs
